name: French Onion Soup- Jarlsberg
recipeIngredient: 4 large Spanish Onions or 8 medium Vidalia Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, heavy Dutch oven, melt the light butter.
2. Once melted, add the sliced onions and fructose (or table sugar).
3. Cover and cook, stirring often, until the onions are tender but colorless.
4. Remove the lid, turn up the heat to high, and continue cooking the onions until they develop a golden caramel color from the sugar.
5. Stir in the flour to form a roux, blending well and cooking over medium heat for three minutes until the starchy flavor of the flour is cooked out.
6. Add the wine and pepper to the onions while stirring over high heat until the mixture comes to a boil and thickens.
7. Pour in the beef broth and bring to a boil. Once boiling, reduce heat and simmer for twenty minutes so the flavors have time to blend.
8. To serve, ladle the soup into small, oven-safe bowls or broiler-safe dishes. Float slices of dried French bread on top, then add a quarter cup of grated Parmesan cheese and a quarter cup of grated Swiss cheese. Finish with a slice of Jarlsberg cheese. Allow the cheese to melt slightly under the broiler, watching carefully as it bubbles and turns a rich dark brown.
9. Serve immediately and enjoy!
